title: Lost Things
content:
Oh, I could let the world go by,
Its loud new wonders and its wars,
But how will I give up the sky
When winter dusk is set with stars?

And I could let the cities go,
Their changing customs and their creeds,
But oh, the summer rains that blow
In silver on the jewel-weeds!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Sara Teasdale delivers a powerful performance in "Lost Things"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
